summary: High‑energy summer ’91 show with scorching Scarlet > Fire and soaring Comes A Time, captured on a crisp soundboard recording.
review: The performance is widely praised for its blazing second set – especially the marathon Scarlet > Fire and an emotive Comes A Time – plus strong moments from Candyman, Memphis Blues and Jack Straw > Sugaree.  The SBD source is generally described as clear and “top‑notch,” though a few listeners note occasional keyboard over‑mixing or a low Jerry level.  Overall the show feels like one of the standout Summer ’91 concerts.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (20+) give 4‑5 stars and highlight strong playing and excellent sound; only a few note mix issues, giving an overall very positive impression.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, top‑notch SBD capture praised by many; occasional complaints about keyboards drowning the mix and one note of Jerry being low in the mix, but no major dropouts or distortion reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows hot and steady – a vibrant first set that builds into an epic second set, highlighted by the long Scarlet > Fire segue and a heartfelt Comes A Time emerging out of space.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for fiery solo on Scarlet > Fire, clear vocals and energetic playing; one reviewer mentions his mix being low but overall he’s the driving force.
band_member_comments.Phil: Only minor mention (standing by during Good Lovin'); no strong positive or negative comments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Negative note about his performance of Good Lovin' that irritated Jerry; otherwise not highlighted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mixed – several reviewers criticize Bruce Hornsby’s accordion/keyboard over‑mix, calling it “kinda fuck it up” and even saying Vince “sucks,” while a few simply note the dual‑keyboard swirl as characteristic of the era.
band_member_comments.Drums: Positive nods: drums highlighted as a highlight for rhythm lovers and praised in at least one review.
